text:
Jack liked to climb trees. One day he climbed up Branch Owl.

"Hello," chirped the owl. "What big owl little thing you there?"

"A big person," whispered Jack.

"You're kind." The owl hooted happily.

Just then, Fox-face came flying across a field. "Hey!"

Jack looked down.

"Oh!" Fox-face swooped down. "That's no bird. That's just my uncle Jack!"

He hooted proudly. Always liked big owls.

"Hi! I'm Fox."

"It's Fox." They hooted together.

"You must be tired." Jack watched his fluffy tail.

"A bit."

"That's cool." Jack yawned. Then.

"Wake up sleepyhead!"

Fox hooted impatiently.

"Just... go back to sleepy."

"Fancy flying with me?"

"No."

"You're cool." Jack smiled.

They hooted.

When night came.

"What cool/owl thing do you want?"

Just the moon.

"Aw
